name: Agent: Email Development
summary: Turns a Figma design into an email that looks right in every inbox — including the ones that break everything.
objectives: Turn a Figma design into an email that looks right in every inbox — including the ones that break everything.
Stop a broken email before it sends, with a check that runs as code instead of a person squinting at it.

outcomes.text: Anyone can download the agent and build an email with it. There is a guide that walks through it, step by step.

The inboxes that break the most — Outlook on Windows, Outlook.com, Gmail in dark mode — now each have a recipe that works, tested on real renders. Every attempt is written down, including the ones that failed and the limits still being lived with.

evidence.label: Dark mode: what worked, what didn't

outcomes.text: The pre-send checklist runs as code. It reads the finished email, stops the send if a rule is broken, and writes up what it found.

evidence.label: The check that blocks a bad send

outcomes.text: Design to email is a written ten-step process, not one person's habit — so a second person gets the same result as the first.

It built a whole newsletter for a brand it had never seen — different colours, a different font, a different language and layout — from its generic rules alone. It passed, and the two gaps that showed up became new rules.
One email pipeline instead of two. The old system was retired and the agent's template became the only source of real email, so nobody has to ask which of the two is right.

milestones.title: Put everything the team knew about email into one portable set of rules
Retire the old system, so there is one pipeline instead of two
Make the agent's template the only source of real email
Turn the pre-send checklist into code that blocks a bad send
Prove it end to end and turn production back on
Prove it works for a brand it has never seen
Publish the agent and its guide, so anyone can use it
Give the team a way to draft and check an email without running code
